YAML Line Parser Module\n//!\n//! This module provides the core data structures for representing parsed YAML lines.\n//! It defines types that hold line-by-line parsing results, including line type,\n//! indentation level, and raw content.\n//!\n//! ## Architecture\n//!\n//! The line parser module provides the foundational types for line-based YAML analysis:\n//!\n//! - [`LineType`] - Enumeration of all possible YAML line types\n//! - [`YamlLine`] - Struct representing a single parsed YAML line\n//! - [`LineContent`] - Structured content representation for different line types\n//!\n//! ## Usage\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::line_parser::{YamlLine, LineType};\n//!\n//! let line = YamlLine::new(1, \"key: value\", 0, LineType::MappingKey);\n//! assert_eq!(line.line_number(), 1);\n//! assert_eq!(line.raw_content(), \"key: value\");\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Line Type Categories\n//!\n//! - **Structural**: Blank lines, document markers\n//! - **Comments**: Full and inline comments\n//! - **Content**: Mapping keys, values, sequence items\n//! - **Special**: Anchors, aliases, tags, directives\n\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Classification of YAML line types\n///\n/// This enum provides structured categorization of different line types\n/// that can occur in YAML files. Each variant represents a specific semantic\n/// category recognized by the YAML specification.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum LineType {\n    /// Empty line (only whitespace or no characters)\n    Blank,\n\n    /// Comment line (starts with #)\n    Comment,\n\n    /// Document start marker (---)\n    DocumentStart,\n\n    /// Document end marker (...)\n    DocumentEnd,\n\n    /// Mapping key line (e.g., \"key:\" or \"key: value\")\n    MappingKey,\n\n    /// Mapping value line (value after colon on same line)\n    MappingValue,\n\n    /// Sequence item line (starts with \"- \")\n    SequenceItem,\n\n    /// Flow style mapping (starts with {)\n    FlowMapping,\n\n    /// Flow style sequence (starts with [)\n    FlowSequence,\n\n    /// Anchor definition (starts with &)\n    Anchor,\n\n    /// Alias reference (starts with *)\n    Alias,\n\n    /// Tag directive (starts with !)\n    Tag,\n\n    /// YAML directive (starts with %)\n    Directive,\n\n    /// Literal block scalar (starts with |)\n    LiteralBlockScalar,\n\n    /// Folded block scalar (starts with >)\n    FoldedBlockScalar,\n\n    /// Unrecognized content (fallback for unknown patterns)\n    Unknown,\n}\n\nimpl LineType {\n    /// Get a human-readable description of this line type\n    pub fn description(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Blank => \"blank line\",\n            Self::Comment => \"comment line\",\n            Self::DocumentStart => \"document start marker\",\n            Self::DocumentEnd => \"document end marker\",\n            Self::MappingKey => \"mapping key\",\n            Self::MappingValue => \"mapping value\",\n            Self::SequenceItem => \"sequence item\",\n            Self::FlowMapping => \"flow style mapping\",\n            Self::FlowSequence => \"flow style sequence\",\n            Self::Anchor => \"anchor definition\",\n            Self::Alias => \"alias reference\",\n            Self::Tag => \"tag directive\",\n            Self::Directive => \"YAML directive\",\n            Self::LiteralBlockScalar => \"literal block scalar\",\n            Self::FoldedBlockScalar => \"folded block scalar\",\n            Self::Unknown => \"unknown content\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this line type is a structural element\n    ///\n    /// Structural elements are blank lines, document markers, and comments\n    /// that don't contribute to the actual data content.\n    pub fn is_structural(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(\n            self,\n            Self::Blank | Self::DocumentStart | Self::DocumentEnd | Self::Comment\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this line type is meaningful content\n    ///\n    /// Meaningful content includes keys, values, and data items.\n    pub fn is_content(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(\n            self,\n            Self::MappingKey |\n            Self::MappingValue |\n            Self::SequenceItem |\n            Self::FlowMapping |\n            Self::FlowSequence |\n            Self::LiteralBlockScalar |\n            Self::FoldedBlockScalar\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this line type is a YAML directive or special marker\n    pub fn is_directive(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Directive | Self::Tag | Self::Anchor | Self::Alias)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all line type variants\n    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {\n        &[\n            Self::Blank,\n            Self::Comment,\n            Self::DocumentStart,\n            Self::DocumentEnd,\n            Self::MappingKey,\n            Self::MappingValue,\n            Self::SequenceItem,\n            Self::FlowMapping,\n            Self::FlowSequence,\n            Self::Anchor,\n            Self::Alias,\n            Self::Tag,\n            Self::Directive,\n            Self::LiteralBlockScalar,\n            Self::FoldedBlockScalar,\n            Self::Unknown,\n        ]\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for LineType {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{}\", self.description())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Structured content representation for different line types\n///\n/// This enum provides typed access to the semantic content of a parsed line,\n/// allowing type-safe extraction of line-specific data.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub enum LineContent {\n    /// No content (blank lines, document markers)\n    Empty,\n\n    /// Comment content (text after #)\n    Comment(String),\n\n    /// Mapping key content (key name and optional value)\n    MappingKey {\n        /// The key name\n        key: String,\n        /// The value, if present on the same line\n        value: Option<String>,\n    },\n\n    /// Sequence item content\n    SequenceItem {\n        /// The item value (after the \"- \" prefix)\n        value: String,\n    },\n\n    /// Flow style content","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":2735}}}

Core parser trait and implementations for YAML parsing\n//!\n//! This module defines the Parser trait and provides functionality for\n//! parsing YAML content from various sources.\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::{\n    types::{ParseResult, ValidationResult, ValidationError},\n    error::ParseError,\n    ParserConfig,\n    syntax_validator::SyntaxValidator,\n    syntax_detector::SyntaxDetector,\n    scope::ScopeStack,\n    line_parser::{calculate_indentation},\n    scope::extract_key_context,\n};\n\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::debug as log_debug;\n\n/// Trait for YAML parsers\n///\n/// This trait defines the core interface for parsing YAML content.\n/// Implementations can parse from strings, files, or other sources.\npub trait Parser {\n    /// Parse YAML content from a string\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content as a string\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A ParseResult containing the parsed data or an error\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a byte slice\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a file\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Validate YAML content without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Validate a YAML file without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Get the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A reference to the parser's configuration\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig;\n\n    /// Set the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `config` - The new configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// The parser with the new configuration\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized;\n}\n\n/// Basic YAML parser implementation\n///\n/// This is a minimal implementation of the Parser trait that\n/// provides basic YAML parsing functionality with scope-aware key tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct BasicParser {\n    config: ParserConfig,\n    /// Scope stack for tracking keys within their proper scope contexts\n    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n}\n\nimpl BasicParser {\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with default configuration\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with the specified configuration\n    pub fn with_config(config: ParserConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new strict parser\n    ///\n    /// A strict parser enables strict mode and disallows duplicate keys.\n    /// Uses the comprehensive strict configuration from ParserConfig.\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::strict(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n    ///\n    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n                }\n                scope_stack.reset();\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip YAML directives\n            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Handle blank lines and comments\n            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                // Track blank lines with different indentation for debugging, but don't\n                // change scope. Scope transitions only happen on content lines.\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                if indent != scope_stack.current_indent() && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Blank line with indent change detected: line={}, indent={}, current_indent={}, skipping (will handle on next content line)\",\n                        line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.current_indent());\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                            }\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n                        // - A scalar value continuation\n                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                        {\n                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Less => {\n                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1388}}}

trigger\n350\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n351\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n352\t                // Track blank lines with different indentation for debugging, but don't\n353\t                // change scope. indentation changes\n363\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n364\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n365\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n366\t YAML parser module\n//!\n//! This module provides YAML parsing functionality for the ARMOR project.\n//! It includes types for parsing results, error handling, and the main parser trait.\n//!\n//! ## Architecture\n//!\n//! The YAML parser module is organized into several sub-modules:\n//!\n//! - [`error`] - Comprehensive error types and error handling\n//! - [`types`] - Result types for parsing operations\n//! - [`parser`] - YAML-specific parser trait and implementations\n//! - [`syntax_validator`] - Syntax validation functionality\n//! - [`syntax_detector`] - Syntax error detection (indentation, delimiters, structure)\n//! - [`line_parser`] - Core data structures for representing parsed YAML lines\n//! - [`scope`] - Hierarchical scope representation for duplicate key detection\n//!\n//! ## Quick Start\n//!\n//! ```no_run\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::{parse_yaml, ParseResult};\n//!\n//! fn main() {\n//!     let yaml = r#\"\n//!         name: example\n//!         value: 42\n//!     \"#;\n//!\n//!     let result = parse_yaml(yaml);\n//!     if result.is_success() {\n//!         println!(\"Parsed successfully\");\n//!     }\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Line Parser\n//!\n//!
